signals.rationale: Apple makes stuff people want to buy. Right now money is expensive, so investors want savings instead of stocks. Not a good time to buy, but okay to keep if you own it.
Johnson & Johnson is a stable company that makes essential products. People will always need their medicines, so it's a good investment even when interest rates are high.
Microsoft is a strong company, but its stock price is a bit high. With interest rates up, it might not be the best time to buy, but if you own it, it's okay to keep.
NVIDIA's stock price has gone up a lot, and with high interest rates, it might be too expensive. It's not a good time to buy, and you might want to wait for the price to come down.
JPMorgan Chase is a big bank that does well when interest rates are high. They can make more money from loans, so it's a good time to invest in them.
UnitedHealth Group is a solid company, but its stock price is a bit high. With moderate inflation, people might still need health insurance, so it's okay to keep if you own it.
Bank of America is a big bank that can make more money when interest rates are high. They're also paying a good dividend, so it's a good time to invest in them.
Alphabet's stock price is a bit high, and with high interest rates, investors might prefer safer options. It's not a good time to buy, and you might want to wait for the price to come down.
Pfizer is a stable company that makes essential medicines. People will always need their products, so it's a good investment even when interest rates are high.
Meta is a big company, but its stock price has been volatile. With moderate inflation and low volatility, it's okay to keep if you own it, but not a great time to buy.
